name: Financial Modeling Prep API
what_it_does: Provides a comprehensive REST API for stock prices, historical financial statements, DCF valuations, economic data, and crypto prices spanning 30+ years of history.
use_cases: Build stock screening agents that filter on PE ratios, revenue growth, and debt-to-equity across thousands of tickers
Automate fundamental analysis pipelines pulling income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements
Power DCF valuation models with pre-computed fair value estimates and custom inputs
Monitor earnings calendars and analyst estimates to trigger pre-earnings research workflows
Integrate crypto price feeds alongside equity data for multi-asset portfolio dashboards
not_for: Applications requiring real-time sub-second tick data for high-frequency or algorithmic trading
Teams needing options chain data or Greeks — FMP does not offer derivatives data
best_when: Best when you need deep historical fundamental data (30+ years) at low cost for finance automation, screening agents, or educational finance tools.
avoid_when: Avoid when your agent requires real-time streaming data or options/derivatives data, as FMP is primarily a REST-based historical and delayed data provider.

auth.notes: API key appended as query parameter (apikey=). Community-maintained SDKs handle this transparently. No header-based auth option documented.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 250 requests per day, limited to select endpoints, delayed data only
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $14/month (300 req/min, full endpoints); Professional from $49/month; Enterprise pricing available
pricing.notes: Very affordable entry point for individual developers and small teams. Historical depth and real-time access require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: 250 requests/day on free tier; 300 requests/minute on Starter; higher limits on Professional and Enterprise

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses return HTTP 200 with an error key in the JSON body rather than appropriate HTTP error codes, which complicates agent error detection.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GETs; safe to retry without side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: API returns HTTP 200 even on errors (e.g., invalid ticker, exceeded quota) — agents must inspect the response body for an 'Error Message' field
Free tier daily quota resets at midnight UTC with no warning header before exhaustion
Community SDKs may lag behind API changes; always verify against official docs for new endpoints

security_score_components.security_notes: API key in query string is logged by default in most web servers and proxies — a significant antipattern. No scope or IP restriction options available.
